---
title: "Core objects"
subtitle: "The small set of objects that most neuroim workflows build on."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: tutorials
---

## Spatial metadata

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, str(Path.cwd().parents[1] / "src"))

import numpy as np
import neuroim as ni

space = ni.NeuroSpace((12, 14, 10), spacing=(2.0, 2.0, 2.5), origin=(-12, -14, -10))
space.dim, space.spacing, space.origin
```

## Volumes

```{python}
x, y, z = np.indices(space.dim)
data = x + 0.5 * y + 0.25 * z
vol = ni.DenseNeuroVol(data.astype(float), space)

vol.shape, float(vol.data.mean())
```

## Regions of interest

```{python}
center = np.array(space.dim) // 2
roi = ni.spherical_roi(vol, centroid=center, radius=3)
roi_values = vol[roi.coords[:, 0], roi.coords[:, 1], roi.coords[:, 2]]

int(roi.coords.shape[0]), round(float(roi_values.mean()), 3)
```

## 4D vectors

```{python}
rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
timepoints = 8
series = np.stack(
    [data + 0.1 * t + rng.normal(scale=0.02, size=space.dim) for t in range(timepoints)],
    axis=3,
)
vec = ni.DenseNeuroVec(series, space.add_dim(size=timepoints))

center_series = vec.series(np.array([center])).ravel()
center_series[:4]
```

## File I/O

The main I/O functions are `read_vol()`, `write_vol()`, `read_vec()`, and `write_vec()`. They preserve both array data and spatial metadata for supported formats.

```python
ni.write_vol(vol, "example.nii.gz")
loaded = ni.read_vol("example.nii.gz")
```
